acceptanceCriteria.criterion: ANSI-bearing event canonical bytes match Python rfc8785
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: Lowercase control-escape fixture and canonical hashing regression pass.
Canonicalization and chain rejections enter the bounded local repair path while preserving stable identities.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: All pending bos-front#2 events are confirmed without identity loss
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: Local confirmed watermark is 12614 with receipt 9f9d9fcd72d924ae21a5da4f3d and no pending event or batch files.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Remote run chain verifies from sequence 1 through the recovered tail
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: bos_get_run_history returns audit_sequence_gap. Git audit analysis finds 83 retained-telemetry gaps between sequences 9407 and 9507 in the historically accepted sparse batch 9401-9508.
321 tests, 0 failures, 2 intentional skips, 100 percent coverage, Credo and Dialyzer clean.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Independent reviews and exact-head evidence pass
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: Exact-head implementation is published, but terminal review cannot pass until the historical retention gap has an explicit verifiable contract.

residualRisk.summary: Historical telemetry compaction removed 83 confirmed event bodies without a durable retention-exception proof, so the complete remote chain cannot currently be verified.

summary: Future ANSI and rejection recovery is implemented and fully tested, but the historical BOS Front chain requires a game-api retention-proof repair before #15 can pass.
